verses.text: Babylon's gods Bel and Nebo stoop and are loaded on weary beasts, unable to save even themselves. The idols are a burden. False gods must be carried; they cannot carry their worshippers.
Babylon's gods Bel and Nebo stoop and are loaded on weary beasts, unable to save even themselves. The idols are a burden. False gods must be carried; they cannot carry their worshippers.
By contrast God says He has borne His people from birth: 'even to your old age I am he... I will carry you.' He bears, not is borne. The living God upholds His own through every age of life.
By contrast God says He has borne His people from birth: 'even to your old age I am he... I will carry you.' He bears, not is borne. The living God upholds His own through every age of life.
'To whom will ye liken me?' Men make an idol, carry it, set it up, and it cannot move or answer. The comparison is absurd. No god men fashion can stir to help the one who cries to it.
'To whom will ye liken me?' Men make an idol, carry it, set it up, and it cannot move or answer. The comparison is absurd. No god men fashion can stir to help the one who cries to it.
'To whom will ye liken me?' Men make an idol, carry it, set it up, and it cannot move or answer. The comparison is absurd. No god men fashion can stir to help the one who cries to it.
He alone is God, 'declaring the end from the beginning,' calling 'a ravenous bird from the east' to fulfil His counsel. His purpose stands. The God who foretells and performs governs all the future.

He alone is God, 'declaring the end from the beginning,' calling 'a ravenous bird from the east' to fulfil His counsel. His purpose stands. The God who foretells and performs governs all the future.
He alone is God, 'declaring the end from the beginning,' calling 'a ravenous bird from the east' to fulfil His counsel. His purpose stands. The God who foretells and performs governs all the future.
'Hearken unto me, ye stouthearted'; He brings His righteousness near, and 'my salvation shall not tarry.' Deliverance is sure. God's salvation comes at His appointed time, not a moment late.
'Hearken unto me, ye stouthearted'; He brings His righteousness near, and 'my salvation shall not tarry.' Deliverance is sure. God's salvation comes at His appointed time, not a moment late.
